title: [bug] usage consumer dead-letters valid billing events on any transient DB error
description: apps/usage-service/internal/amqp/consumer.go:135 does Nack(false,false) -> DLQ on EVERY rec.Record error. RecordOnce returns errors for transient conditions too (pool.Begin, tx.Exec, tx.Commit failures: usage-db restart/reset/deadlock/cancelled ctx). A brief usage-db blip dead-letters every event in the window; they are never recorded and never retried -> silent undercharge until someone drains the DLQ. Decode-poison (permanent) and transient-record errors are conflated. Fix: distinguish permanent (decode) from transient (record) -> Nack(requeue=true) or bounded retry for transient, DLQ only poison. Related: #9 (ctx cancel on SIGTERM dead-letters in-flight event) and #6 (Start returns on broker loss, only logged, health stays ok).
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] completeUpload idempotency hole: retry regresses ready->uploaded, reprocesses + double-bills
description: markUploadedForUser (apps/photo-service/src/photo/photo.repository.ts:32) does SET status='uploaded' with NO status guard (only id+userId). A photo already 'ready' that receives a duplicate/retried CompleteUpload (double-click, gRPC retry) is regressed ready->uploaded (disappears from ready/ListPhotoSpacetime views); markProcessingForUser (repo:119, guarded on status='uploaded') then matches again -> new processing_jobs row + new photo.process job -> media-worker reprocesses -> finalizeResult emits usage under the NEW jobId (charge-once key is jobId, not photoId) -> duplicate processing/variant billing + status corruption. The comment at photo.service.ts:101-106 asserts a no-op invariant that is false because markUploadedForUser at line 91 resets status first. Fix: guard the uploaded transition (e.g. only uploading->uploaded) so a completed/ready photo is not reopened; make the whole complete flow idempotent per photoId.
close_reason: Closed

title: Clustering: decompose method into algorithm × feature-space + add HDBSCAN (time-only)
description:
User request (post-019): the current single time_only method (threshold-based device-segmented agglomerative) is 'not awful but insufficient' — a fixed merge threshold doesn't adapt to density. Two asks:

1) Add HDBSCAN as a time-only algorithm (density-based; adapts to variable density, handles noise). New ClusteringMethod subclass; interface fits as-is. Needs a dep (scikit-learn HDBSCAN or the hdbscan package; scipy is present, sklearn is not).

2) Separate ALGORITHM from FEATURE-SPACE so {time-only, space-time} × {agglomerative, hdbscan, ...} are orthogonal options, not one conflated 'method' id. Recommended model (B/C): method = algorithm; feature-space is a separate axis (param in params_json or a new descriptor field) that selects the metric (1D time vs time+haversine). Rejected: register the cross-product as flat ids (combinatorial, doesn't express orthogonality).

Architecture ALREADY supports this: ClusteringMethod ABC + registry (methods/base.py, subclass+register), MethodDescriptor carries required_photo_fields + default_params (surfaced by ListClusteringMethods → ClusterView picker), PhotoPoint already carries lat/lon ('for the future space-time method'). space-time is a documented seam.

Constraints for the brainstorm:
- Determinism (ADR-0005): deterministic in input+method+params; HDBSCAN is deterministic given fixed input order — guard it.
- merge_distance currently = dendrogram merge height (agglomerative); HDBSCAN's lambda/stability doesn't map 1:1 — decide null vs reinterpret.
- space-time metric needs NUMERIC lat/lon (photo-service already extracts EXIF GPS), NOT human-readable Location (that's geo-normalization, photo_ops-3iy / session 022). So space-time clustering is technically NOT blocked on 3iy; GPS-less photos fall into not_clusterable (expected). But natural to co-land with 022 to avoid doing the registry/axis work twice.

Sequencing: stage 5 (clustering); do in/around session 022 (geo/space-time), after 020 (share/polish). Related: photo_ops-3iy (geo-normalization).

title: Publication hardening: UpdatePost input validation + node-selection guards (s017 review follow-ups)
description:
Deferred findings from the session-017 final /code-review (publication-service). These harden not-yet-UI-exercised paths and belong with the 018 editor + 018 create-post affordance that own the update/selection UX.

1. PATCH visibility: an unknown visibility string (typo) currently maps to undefined and is silently dropped -> 200 with no change (privacy-relevant no-op). Validate at the gateway (whitelist private|unlisted|public) -> 400.
2. PATCH date_from/date_to: an invalid/non-ISO string becomes Invalid Date and reaches Drizzle (500 on toISOString, or corrupt row). Validate/parse at the edge -> 400; decide date-only vs instant semantics.
3. CreatePostFromCluster node selection: findNode matches ANY node id. Posting the ROOT node snapshots the whole tree incl. the not_clusterable bucket; an empty/not_clusterable node yields a silently-empty 0-photo post. Add a guard (reject empty subtree and/or non-selectable node kinds) once m71.2 defines which nodes are user-selectable.
4. Gateway mapPost/mapSummary spread ...raw -> shape an explicit browser DTO instead (matches cluster/photo controllers; stops future proto fields auto-leaking). Note: smoke currently asserts sourceClusterId/sourceResultId, so keep those if intentionally exposed.
5. Defensive: collectPhotos has no dedup; a photo_id at two nodes would abort create on the post_photos PK. Contract guarantees single-entry today, so this is defensive only.
6. Strengthen smoke-publication UpdatePost assertion: verify seeded dates + snapshotted photos survive a title-only PATCH (guards partial-update regressions).

Origin: 8-angle high-effort review of session-017-publication-foundation; the create-path error mapping + owner-scope smoke + schema index drift were fixed in-session; these were deferred as 018-editor-owned hardening.

title: [epic] Publication vertical: cluster -> draft post -> publish -> public page -> share (DoD steps 8-13)
description:
Close the missing RIGHT HALF of the MVP vertical. Today the flow dead-ends at viewing a cluster tree; publication-service and connector-service are 501 health-only stubs, and there is no Post model, gateway route, editor, or public page. This epic delivers the product's headline capability (a published public photo story) as a THIN slice.

Thin-slice guardrails (Principle 3, ship the thinnest real path):
- publication-service becomes a real NestJS gРПc service owning publication-db (Post, PostPhoto). Mirror photo-service structure.
- Reuse identity session auth via api-gateway (userId from validated session); UUID v7 cross-service refs, no cross-service FK.
- Public delivery uses prepared photo VARIANTS (preview/publish), never originals (architecture invariant + §4.4 privacy).
- Deliberately cut for the first slice: Telegram connector (copy-link only), map (map_enabled stored but public page can defer rendering), notes, split/merge. These stay seams.

Child steps (see linked issues) map to DoD 8-13:

  10 post editor: title/body/captions/order
  11-12 publish -> public /posts/[slug] page
  13 shareable link (copy-link; Telegram deferred)

Definition of done for the epic: from a ready cluster a user creates a draft, edits it, publishes it, opens the public URL in a logged-out browser, and copies the share link. Emit post_published usage event (already a defined event type; currently never fires).

title: [bug] media-worker turns transient storage errors into permanent FAILED then acks
description: apps/media-worker/src/media_worker/handler.py:69 blanket except Exception catches transient MinIO/S3 errors (503, connection reset) from download/upload (_process lines 127,136), publishes a permanent PROCESSING_OUTCOME_FAILED and returns normally -> rabbitmq.py acks. Message is gone with no redelivery; photo marked FAILED downstream where a retry would have succeeded. Defeats the idempotent claim-check design (handler should only catch EXPECTED failures). Fix: classify transient vs permanent; nack/requeue (or let it raise for redelivery) on transient storage errors, only mark FAILED on genuinely permanent (corrupt/unsupported image) failures.

title: [bug] cluster-worker re-inserts a second tree on redelivery-while-pending (same class as 1m8)
description: apps/cluster-service/src/cluster_service/store_postgres.py:30 save_tree guards only on status='pending' and does NOT flip status to ready (the server's cluster.result consumer does). The worker recomputes the tree with FRESH uuid7 node ids each run. If a cluster.process message is redelivered (worker crashed after publishing SUCCEEDED but before ack) any time before the server flips the run to ready, save_tree sees 'pending' again and runs _insert_node a second time -> two parent_id IS NULL roots under one result_id; _build_tree picks one root and orphans the other -> corrupted result. Window is the whole pending->ready gap. Fix: make save_tree idempotent (e.g. clear/skip if nodes already exist for result_id, or key node ids deterministically off result_id+path). Sibling of open bug photo_ops-1m8 (no-pending-row branch of the same guard).

title: [bug] finalizeResult non-atomic: crash strands photo in 'processing' forever
description: apps/photo-service/src/photo/photo.service.ts:177 finalizeJob(jobId,'succeeded') (guarded on status='queued') commits first, then upsertVariant/applyAttributes/setStatus('ready') run as separate non-transactional statements. A crash after the job commit but before setStatus('ready') nacks to DLQ; any redelivery finds the job no longer 'queued' -> finalizeJob returns false -> finalizeResult early-returns at line 179 -> variants/attributes/ready never applied. Photo permanently stuck at 'processing'. Fix: apply variants+attributes+status in one transaction with the job finalization, or make finalize idempotent so redelivery re-applies the terminal state.

title: exSDD: coverage gate on skeleton review-readiness (new/changed code must be RED-tested)
description:
Gap in the exSDD process (docs/agent-workflow-evolution.md Decision 1): the skeleton IS "the spec, reviewed as a unit", but nothing mechanically verifies that every new stub/signature has a covering RED test before hand-off to review. Recurring defect: s012 shipped internal/usage/reader.go as a stub with NO RED test (see photo_ops-osq); recent runs generally produced "spec without tests". Principle: code expresses intent, tests express expected behavior -> a skeleton missing tests is missing half the spec.

What: make a coverage check a HARD review-readiness gate on the skeleton. Every new/changed function/branch the skeleton introduces must be exercised by a skeleton RED test (no untested new stubs). Prefer new/changed-code coverage over a global %, so pre-existing untested code does not block. On fail -> skeleton is NOT review-ready; return to author to add the missing RED test (spec-change protocol).

Boundaries (no duplicate truth): photo_ops-osq = the MEASUREMENT tooling (per-language coverage + combined report). THIS issue = the PROCESS OBLIGATION + gate wiring that consumes it. Home for the rule follows photo_ops-dqb's decision and the plugin-extraction issue (skill and/or the process plugin). Reference Decision 1 guardrails; do not restate them.

Acceptance: a documented, enforced gate that blocks skeleton->review hand-off when new/changed code lacks a covering test, with new-code scoping and a return-to-rework path.
close_reason: Delivered: make skeleton-gate (RED, 100% new/changed-code, at skeleton->human-review handoff) + make coverage-gate (GREEN, branch/merge + CI PR job); rule in AGENTS.md Workflow Rules; design docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-coverage-gate-design.md. Recorded in agent-workflow-evolution Decision 7.

title: CI Quality gate + make gate: typecheck runs before build, so @photoops/observability (types->dist, gitignored) is unresolved in clean envs
description: Pre-existing (main CI red since ~2026-06-25). In a clean checkout (CI, or local after rm dist) tsc fails TS2307 'Cannot find module @photoops/observability'. It only passes locally because dist lingers from prior builds. Fix options: (a) build the workspace libraries (packages/*) before typecheck in make gate + CI; (b) add a prepare/build step; (c) reorder gate to build->typecheck. title: Adopt executable-spec / skeleton-first SDD (file down writing-plans + subagent-driven-development)
description: Re-aim project-level superpowers per docs/agent-workflow-evolution.md (Decision 1/2): writing-plans emits a skeleton commit (stub signatures + RED tests + short why-note with entry-point links) instead of full implementation; subagent-driven-development brief tells the implementer to make the RED tests green within the stubs, which collapses most per-task spec-compliance review; keep full dual-verdict review only for architecture-sensitive tasks. Requires the project-level superpowers install first. Runs as its own short setup session immediately before 011 (skills reload only at session start), NOT 011's first commit.
notes: Skeleton-first rules (from external review; see docs/agent-workflow-evolution.md Decision 1 Skeleton guardrails). writing-plans emits a skeleton (stubs + RED tests + WHY note + non-goals), not code. RED tests constrain obligations not the route; each test has explicit fixture + expected output + one reason it matters. subagent-driven-development brief: implementer makes RED tests green and may add narrower tests, but may NOT weaken, delete, or rename skeleton tests; if a skeleton test is wrong, stop and run the spec-change protocol (note which artifact must change, get approval, update skeleton, re-run RED). green + typecheck = task-local verdict only; global verdict stays at branch level (final whole-branch review + integration tests 4vg). Skeleton must stay reviewable: about 1-3 acceptance tests + a few focused tests + minimal stubs + contract/schema diff + short WHY; an 800-line skeleton is unfinished implementation. Roles author/implementer/reviewer are hats one agent wears + the final-review subagent, not three agents per task.
close_reason: Napilnik skill-edit pass complete: writing-plans -> skeleton-first (stubs + RED tests + WHY note, not full code); subagent-driven-development -> implementer brief = make RED tests green in the stubs, conditional per-task review (green+typecheck task-local; full dual-verdict only for architecture-sensitive), guarded skeleton tests + spec-change protocol. Plugin de-embedded/vendored (45e3ea1) + methodology edits (866e197) on feat/657-skeleton-first-sdd. Edits take effect in the next fresh session (011).

title: Make live-stack validation first-class: one-shot make smoke-stack
description: s008's biggest lesson: a green 'make gate' AND a clean whole-branch review missed THREE real bugs (worker RabbitMQ boot-race crash, the _lat/_lon proto3-optional leak, the smoke's own assertion) that ONLY the live 'make smoke-media' caught -- mocks share the code's assumptions, the real transport does not. Make the highest-value validation cheap and reliable: a 'make smoke-stack' target that (a) df headroom check, (b) builds only the services the smoke needs, (c) up -d + wait for health, (d) make migrate, (e) make smoke-media, (f) teardown -- with full build output to a LOG FILE, not '| tail' (which ate the failure in s008). Also dodges the detached-build-log-loss and ENOSPC frictions (#3/#4). See docs/agent-ergonomics.md (s008 friction #8).
close_reason: Added 'make smoke-stack' (scripts/smoke-stack.sh): df pre-check -> build only media-path services -> infra healthy -> migrate -> app up -> functional mesh-readiness probe -> live smoke -> teardown, with full build output to a LOG FILE (not '| tail') and an honest exit code. Verified end-to-end green; also verified it correctly propagates failure + tears down. Dogfooding caught a readiness-race 500 (static /health vs un-warmed gRPC mesh) — fixed in the harness (migrate-before-app + mesh probe). Synced docs/agent-ergonomics.md; cross-linked photo_ops-de6.

title: Test gates: mutation testing (anti-gaming for assertion quality; polyglot)
description:
Spun out of photo_ops-mp0 during the mp0 brainstorm (2026-07-02): mp0 was scoped down to the mechanical test-integrity diff-guard only. Mutation testing is a separate, heavier capability.

What: run mutation testing to catch tests that assert nothing / are too weak (surviving mutants = weak coverage — the semantic gap the diff-guard cannot see). Polyglot reality: Stryker (TS), mutmut/cosmic-ray (Python), Go has no mature mutation tool. Likely scoped to TS+Python first, or a representative package. Slow — run on-demand / nightly, not per-PR.

Why: q2n coverage gate + mp0 diff-guard close "new code has tests" and "tests not deleted", but neither catches a test that runs the code and asserts nothing. Mutation testing is the anti-gaming complement for assertion quality. Its own session; evaluate value vs cost (esp. Go gap).

title: exSDD: extract the process into a self-contained, project-independent plugin (no project-file refs)
description:
Today the exSDD method is spread across: in-place edits to the project-level superpowers skills (.claude/skills/superpowers/skills/writing-plans, subagent-driven-development), AGENTS.md (## Principles + Workflow Rules), scripts/sdd, and docs/agent-workflow-evolution.md -- and it HARD-REFERENCES this repo's carriers (make gate, make smoke-ui/smoke-stack, bd + bd remember, docs/adr, the Makefile, .beads). That entanglement makes the process non-portable.

Goal: package the whole exSDD process as its own distributable plugin (skills + optional hooks + a generalized sdd script) with ZERO references to project files. Abstract project-specific carriers behind config/conventions the host repo supplies: the gate command, the smoke/e2e command, the task tracker, the why/ADR home, the memory store. The same plugin then drops into any repo unchanged; it also becomes a shareable portfolio artifact (aligns with the process-to-product framing and the spun-out A/B experiment repo in Decision 6 / the experiment section).

Note this EVOLVES Decision 1's "napilnik = edit superpowers skills in place at project level": that pinned the method to this repo for diffability; the new goal is the opposite -- decouple it into a standalone plugin. Design must resolve what stays repo-local (the concrete Makefile/bd wiring = a thin host-adapter/config) vs what moves to the portable plugin (the method, skills, gates, sdd verbs). Sibling process issues (photo_ops-dqb rules, photo_ops-gwh routing, photo_ops-a2f sdd verbs, photo_ops-q2n coverage gate, photo_ops-cev review gate) should land IN this plugin, not as more project-local prose. Likely epic / session-sized.

Acceptance: an installable exSDD plugin containing the process (skills/hooks/sdd) with no hard-coded references to photo_ops files; project-specific carriers injected via configuration; a second repo can consume it unchanged.

title: exSDD: automated machine-review gate before human review (pass->human, fail->return spec)
description:
Process improvement to the review loop (docs/agent-workflow-evolution.md Decision 2). Before any artifact (skeleton/spec, and/or the final branch) reaches a HUMAN reviewer, a separate reviewer agent runs an automated machine review; only on a clean pass does it go to the human; on findings it is RETURNED to the author for rework (loop), not forwarded. Goal: humans spend attention only on artifacts that already cleared machine-checkable issues.

Relationship to photo_ops-41q (no duplicate truth): 41q chooses the TOOL for the final whole-branch review (native /code-review). THIS issue adds the GATING STAGE + reject->rework loop as a hard precondition to human hand-off, and applies it to the skeleton review (Decision 1's relocated checkpoint), reusing the same reviewer. They compose: 41q supplies the reviewer; this wires it as a gate.

Watch-out (Decision 2 evidence, arXiv:2505.16222): LLM judges rubber-stamp code that asserts its own correctness. The machine gate must include INDEPENDENT, non-self-attesting checks (builds/typechecks, RED tests actually run, the coverage gate from photo_ops-q2n) -- not just an LLM opinion. Right-size per the two lanes (mechanical vs architecture-sensitive) so we do not over-invest review on mechanical skeletons.

Acceptance: a defined gate where a separate agent reviews before human hand-off, passes clean artifacts through and returns failing ones with actionable findings; scoped by lane; independent (non-self-attesting) checks included.

title: Evaluate OpenMeter/Lago (ready-made metering) vs bespoke usage-service (build-vs-adopt)
description: Principle-1 gap surfaced in s012 retro: we shipped a bespoke thin Go usage ledger (append-only + charge-once inbox + read-time pricing) without formally benchmarking a ready-made usage-metering/billing platform. NOTE: this is NOT about metrics (Prometheus/OTel = pb6) — metrics are lossy/aggregate/low-cardinality/non-idempotent and cannot serve as a billing system-of-record; that's a different, complementary concern. The real 'don't reinvent the wheel' candidates are purpose-built metering platforms: OpenMeter (event ingestion + idempotency/dedup + aggregation + usage API; built on Kafka/ClickHouse) and Lago (open-source billing/metering); hosted: Metronome/Orb. Evaluate build-vs-adopt: what each offloads (ingestion, exactly-once, aggregation, usage API, per-operation reports) vs our bespoke; trade-offs to weigh — infra weight (Kafka/ClickHouse) vs our thin Postgres+RabbitMQ; dependency size + demo/portfolio fit; control over our specific model (provider-independent RAW units + provenance + pricing-resolved-at-read, ADR-0004); migration cost from the current ledger + the usage.events contract (013 depends on it). Trigger to revisit: if usage scope grows toward real pay-as-you-go billing at scale, or when the pricing/rate-card seams (versioned/multi-provider, ADR-0004) come due. Outcome: a keep-bespoke vs adopt-platform decision with rationale (record as an ADR).

title: Bill RAM in byte_seconds (memory integral, self-metered during operations)
description: Owner feedback (s012 add-on review): RAM consumption of an operation should be billed as byte_seconds — the integral of memory used over the operation's duration — self-measured by the operation runtime (same mechanism as the cpu_seconds task). Emitted as resource=memory / unit=byte_second. Pricing resolves $/byte_second. See ADR-0004 raw-unit seam.

title: Bill storage in byte_seconds (storage-reconciler integral over stored objects)
description: Owner feedback (s012 add-on review): storage should be billed as byte_seconds — bytes stored integrated over time (rent), not the current one-shot bytes-stored level (photo_original_stored / unit=byte). Implement the storage-reconciler (ADR-0004 / TZ §5 Go shell) that periodically emits the byte_seconds integral for live objects (and handles deletes). Pricing resolves $/byte_second (≈ $/GB-month). Replaces the demo bytes-level rent estimate.

title: Bill processing in cpu_seconds (self-metered), not an operation count
description: Owner feedback (s012 add-on review): processing consumption is currently emitted as photo_processed / resource=processing / unit=operation (a count, by photo-service). It should be a provider-independent raw unit: cpu_seconds (and likely wall_seconds), self-measured by the operation's runtime (media-worker for media processing; cluster compute already self-meters per s013). Replaces/augments the operation count as the billable processing measure. Pricing resolves $/cpu_second. See ADR-0004 raw-unit seam.

title: scripts/sdd start/ship: intent-verbs over bd+git lifecycle
description:
Extend scripts/sdd with intent-verbs that wrap the bd + git lifecycle deterministically: the agent expresses intent and automation handles mechanics in correct order, no skipped steps, no drift. The git-lifecycle analogue of skeleton-first: turn the session-close protocol from prose-to-follow into a command-to-run, which for an agent is a determinism guarantee against LLM drift.

start <id> (took into work): bd update <id> --claim (status in_progress); derive branch name from the issue (session/NNN-slug or feat/id-slug); git switch -c branch from main.

ship <id> (handed in): make gate as a HARD precondition (red gate refuses); bd close <id>; git switch main; git merge --no-ff branch (local); tag; bd dolt push; then STOP for the human/guard go on git push to main.

Invariants: gate-before-merge; bd status in sync; dolt push before git push (the s008/s010 ordering bug class); correct branch name; no skipped steps.

Hard guard: do NOT automate merge+push to the default branch. The auth-guard blocks it deliberately as a review gate; ship stops at branch-ready / waiting-for-go. The human checkpoint relocates, it does not vanish.

Rejected: git-flow (model mismatch, author-deprecated for continuous-delivery); git-town (only the git half, no beads tie). Repo-local helper wins: zero deps, agent-native, ties bd-status + git in one verb.

Right-size: high recurrence (every session), passes the agent-ergonomics bar; session-sized, behind the process-to-product cap. Do as its own tooling session, e.g. after photo_ops-4vg or when branch ceremony starts costing time. See docs/agent-workflow-evolution.md.

What: keep a subset of acceptance tests HIDDEN from the implementer subagent so it cannot overfit/game to the visible tests; the hidden set runs only at review/branch level. Why: with executable skeletons, an implementer sees the RED tests and could implement narrowly to them. A hidden acceptance set is the classic countermeasure. Process experiment; design must address where hidden tests live, how they run, and how they avoid leaking into the implementer's context.

value: s013 cluster-service deploy shape: monolingual Python, two roles from ONE image/package (apps/cluster-service): entrypoint 'server' = gRPC API + cluster.result consumer + ConsumptionEvent emitter; entrypoint 'worker' = cluster.process consumer (compute+persist). Shared db/codec/metric modules. Mirrors photo-service<->media-worker. Self-metering INCLUDES byte_seconds via RSS sampler (memory-time integral) in this slice, plus cpu_seconds/wall_seconds + domain counter.

s013 cluster-service is the FIRST Python gRPC service. Generated grpc-python stubs (buf.gen.cluster-python.yaml) use imports rooted at the proto ROOT: 'from cluster.v1 import ...', 'from common.v1 import ...', 'from google.api import annotations_pb2'. Packaging requirements: (1) put apps/cluster-service/src/photoops_proto on sys.path (pytest pythonpath=['.','src/photoops_proto']; Dockerfile PYTHONPATH) and import proto as 'cluster.v1.cluster_service_pb2' etc (NOT media-worker's 'src.photoops_proto.X' style — that only works for cross-import-free files); (2) dep googleapis-common-protos supplies google.api.annotations_pb2 (do NOT vendor-generate google/api); (3) dep grpcio for the _pb2_grpc modules. Verified import chain: ClusterServiceServicer/Stub + PhotoServiceStub load.

Usage-accounting contract (s012, usage-service Go): operations emit a ConsumptionEvent proto (proto/usage/v1/consumption.proto) to RabbitMQ exchange 'usage.events' (canonical durable+DLX/DLQ topology). Fields: idempotency_key (charge-once), user_id, provider (producer's provenance, from env), occurred_at (ISO/RFC3339), repeated Measurement{event_type,resource_type,quantity int64,unit,source_entity_type,source_entity_id uuid}. usage-service appends RAW units to billing_events (append-only, NO money cols) idempotently via processed_events inbox; pricing resolved at read (ADR-0004). s013 cluster compute self-meters (byte_seconds/cpu/wall + cluster_generated count) and emits here with idempotency_key=result_id. Emit best-effort (don't fail the operation).

git treats [ ] in a pathspec as a glob character-class, so 'git add "apps/web/app/posts/[id]/page.tsx"' (Next.js dynamic-route dirs) matches NOTHING and the whole 'git add' aborts non-zero — silently staging nothing if stderr is hidden (2>/dev/null). Symptom: files you 'added' show as still-modified after commit; the commit only contains what git mv/earlier staged. Fix: use ':(literal)apps/web/app/posts/[id]/page.tsx' pathspec magic, or 'git add -A <dir>/', or escape the brackets. Bit session 019 (public route [id] page); cost a debugging cycle when make gate's proto-check/coverage revealed dropped content.
PhotoOps project rule: do not use git worktrees; use regular git feature branches with git switch -c because worktrees conflict with beads.

Session-completion gotcha (session 011): AGENTS.md mandates 'git pull --rebase' at session end, but the workflow-evolution directive mandates 'git merge --no-ff' into main to preserve topology. These CONFLICT: a plain 'git pull --rebase' after a local --no-ff merge DROPS the merge commit and flattens history (rebase doesn't preserve merges without --rebase-merges), orphaning any tag placed on the merge commit. In 011 this flattened the --no-ff merge and orphaned tag exp/exec-spec-011; recovered by re-pointing the tag onto main tip + force-updating the remote tag. The 011 commits survive linearly either way (so 'history not lost on branch deletion' still holds). For a future --no-ff session merge to main: either skip the pull --rebase when origin/main hasn't moved (push the merge directly), or use 'git pull --rebase=merges' / rebase before merging, then merge --no-ff and push without a follow-up rebase. Don't force-push main to restore topology after it's pushed.

Project-local superpowers (.claude/skills/superpowers) is VENDORED as plain tracked files, de-embedded from a former gitlink/embedded-repo (commit 45e3ea1, session photo_ops-657). Edit the skill files directly: they diff and commit normally in the parent repo (that was the whole point — Decision 1 action item 1: pin methodology + make it diffable/reviewable). Do NOT re-add a nested .git, do NOT treat it as a git submodule (there is no .gitmodules), and do NOT git submodule update it. Baseline is obra/superpowers v6.0.3 (896224c); the skeleton-first edits to writing-plans + subagent-driven-development are commit 866e197. Skills load at session start, so edits take effect only in a later fresh session.

Buildable workspace package + Docker gotcha (session 010, @photoops/observability): a workspace package that is BUILT (own build script, package.json main: dist/index.js) and consumed at runtime by a service needs TWO Dockerfile things that make gate (no Docker) cannot catch — only make smoke-stack does: (1) the service's pnpm install MUST use the '...' closure (pnpm install --filter "@photoops/<svc>...") so the dependency's devDeps (typescript) install, else its tsc build dies with 'sh: tsc: not found'; the build line already needs the closure to build the dep first; (2) the runtime image MUST contain the package directory (packages/<pkg>/dist) so the workspace symlink node_modules/@photoops/<pkg> -> ../packages/<pkg> resolves at require() time. Single-stage Dockerfiles (whole /repo in image) satisfy (2) for free; a multi-stage image must COPY packages/<pkg> into the runtime stage AND packages/<pkg>/node_modules into the build stage. We converted identity-service from multi-stage to single-stage to match api-gateway/photo-service. Note: source-only packages like proto-ts avoid all this (no build, not imported at runtime).

Next.js 15 dynamic route params are ASYNC: a page for app/.../[id]/page.tsx must be 'async function Page({ params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> })' and 'await params' — a sync {id:string} type FAILS 'next build' (PageProps constraint). jsdom spec renders it via 'render(await Page({ params: Promise.resolve({id}) }))'. First hit in session 018 (posts/[id]/edit — the repo's first dynamic route); future dynamic routes will hit the same.

PhotoOps proto/gRPC + messaging gotchas (session 008): (1) packages/proto-ts is SOURCE-ONLY (main: src/index.ts, no build; imports uninstalled @bufbuild/protobuf) — NOT runtime-consumable. For runtime proto encode/decode use protobufjs loadSync over the .proto (camelCase fields, toObject longs:String->bigint, enums:Number) as in apps/photo-service/src/photo/processing.codec.ts; the Python worker uses generated _pb2 (snake_case). (2) proto3 'optional' compiles to a synthetic '_field' oneof; @grpc/proto-loader surfaces it as '_lat'/'_lon' props that leak through a spread — strip underscore-prefixed keys in the gateway mapPhoto. (3) Both RabbitMQ adapters (TS amqplib + Python pika) connect at startup with bounded retry to survive the broker boot race; canonical topology (exchange/queue/DLX/DLQ, dlq routing-key=N) is mirrored EXACTLY on both sides — change both together or assertQueue fails PRECONDITION_FAILED.

When adding a usage.events emitter to a TS service that is NOT already a RabbitMQ consumer (e.g. publication-service in s019), do NOT copy photo-service's eager RABBITMQ_BUS async factory: RabbitMqBus.create() retries then throws, and because a root provider (PostDomainService) injects the emitter, the bus resolves at NestFactory boot — a down broker crash-loops the whole service and takes every RPC offline (incl. non-emitting public reads). Make the publisher lazy/non-throwing (connect on first emit, guard each emit) so usage stays a true best-effort side-channel. photo-service's boot-coupling is acceptable only because it is inherently a usage.events consumer.

Knowledge placement: AGENTS.md = canonical agent rules; root CLAUDE.md = thin Claude-Code pointer; nested CLAUDE.md (## Local context / ## Local invariants) = local code knowledge for real services + key dirs, stubs for scaffolds; bd remember = durable file-independent facts; docs/adr + docs/superpowers = decisions and per-session design. Nested CLAUDE.md freshness is discipline (update in the same commit), not automated.

Structured logging (zg6): @photoops/observability holds the propagation-only OTel bootstrap (startTracing), pino options with the single REDACT_PATHS list + trace_id/span_id mixin, traceparent bridge helpers (currentTraceparent/withExtractedContext), and GrpcLoggingInterceptor. TS services use nestjs-pino. The async hop carries the FULL W3C traceparent in the proto correlation_id field (photo-service stamps it on publish, re-binds on result consume); job_id stays the idempotency key. media-worker logs trace_id parsed from correlation_id with no Python OTel SDK (that is pb6). No exporter/backend — propagation + log correlation only.

Prioritization principle: invest in tooling that pays off on the existing TypeScript slice now (CI, typecheck, eslint, structured logs); defer anything that depends on not-yet-built Go cluster-service or Python media-worker until those services gain real behavior.

Decision (ADR-0002), not yet implemented: pin the toolchain with mise (.tool-versions) for node/pnpm/go/python; buf stays a pnpm devDependency. JS toolchain is hermetic via pnpm; runtime/integration infra runs in Docker. Planned single canonical 'make bootstrap'. See docs/adr/0002-toolchain-pinning-with-mise.md.

Rebuilding one compose service with 'docker compose ... up -d --build web' recreates its dependency chain (photo/publication/gateway) and can desync the cluster-service<->cluster-worker pair (cluster result 'failed', gRPC UNAVAILABLE Connection refused to worker:50051). Fix: 'docker compose ... up -d --force-recreate cluster-worker cluster-service' before running clustering-dependent smokes (smoke-publication/smoke-ui). Also: docker build cache can fill the disk (ENOSPC mid-build) — 'docker builder prune -af' reclaims it.
